deviations.description: The requirement now obligates discharge approval for patients with more than 5 medications, but the process model does not include any activity or gateway that checks or enforces this condition. The process does not ensure compliance for this new precondition.

The requirement now obligates that, for patients over 65 or with more than 5 medications, a doctor must approve discharge, but the process model only includes 'Approve Patient Discharge' in the Nurse lane and does not include a doctor approval activity. The process does not ensure doctor approval before discharge.
The process model only includes a nurse approval for discharge and does not require or document a doctor approval, which is now mandated by the updated requirement.

The process model does not distinguish between the resource performing the discharge approval (nurse vs. doctor) and does not enforce that a doctor must perform the approval for the specified cases, as now required.

The deletion of the time constraint (approval within 24 hours prior to discharge) in the requirement removes a restriction, but the process model already enforced this constraint, so its removal does not cause non-compliance.
The extension of the temporal validity period does not affect the process model, as the process is not time-bound in the BPMN.
